questions.question: Explain why plants have supportive tissues in large amounts.
questions.answer: Plants are stationary organisms and must remain upright against gravity. Therefore they contain supportive tissues such as sclerenchyma that provide mechanical strength.

questions.question: Describe the structure and function of meristematic tissue.
questions.answer: Meristematic tissue consists of actively dividing cells with dense cytoplasm and prominent nuclei. These cells help in plant growth by producing new cells.

questions.question: Explain differentiation in plant tissues.
questions.answer: Differentiation is the process by which meristematic cells develop into permanent tissues with specific shapes, sizes and functions.

questions.question: Compare parenchyma and sclerenchyma tissues.
questions.answer: Parenchyma cells are living with thin walls and store food, while sclerenchyma cells are dead with thick lignified walls providing strength.

questions.question: Explain the structure of xylem tissue.
questions.answer: Xylem consists of tracheids, vessels, xylem fibres and xylem parenchyma which help in water transport and mechanical support.

questions.question: Explain the structure of phloem tissue.
questions.answer: Phloem is composed of sieve tubes, companion cells, phloem fibres and phloem parenchyma which transport food in plants.

questions.question: Explain the function of epidermal tissue in plants.
questions.answer: Epidermal tissue protects plants from water loss, mechanical injury and infection. It also regulates gas exchange through stomata.

questions.question: What is the role of stomata in plants?
questions.answer: Stomata help in gaseous exchange and transpiration by allowing carbon dioxide and oxygen to move in and out of leaves.

questions.question: Explain the structure and function of epithelial tissue.
questions.answer: Epithelial tissue is composed of tightly packed cells forming protective coverings and linings of organs and body cavities.

questions.question: Explain the importance of connective tissue.
questions.answer: Connective tissue supports organs, binds body parts together and transports substances like oxygen and nutrients.

questions.question: Differentiate between cartilage and bone.
questions.answer: Cartilage is flexible and found in joints, ear and nose, while bone is hard and forms the framework of the body.

questions.question: Explain the functions of bone tissue.
questions.answer: Bone tissue provides support, protects internal organs and helps in body movement by providing attachment to muscles.

questions.question: Differentiate between tendon and ligament.
questions.answer: Tendons connect muscles to bones while ligaments connect bones to bones and provide joint stability.

questions.question: Explain adipose tissue and its function.
questions.answer: Adipose tissue stores fat and provides insulation and protection to the body.

questions.question: Describe areolar tissue.
questions.answer: Areolar tissue is a connective tissue that fills spaces between organs and supports them.

questions.question: Explain the structure of muscular tissue.
questions.answer: Muscular tissue consists of elongated cells called muscle fibres that contract and relax to produce movement.

questions.question: Differentiate between striated and smooth muscles.
questions.answer: Striated muscles are voluntary and attached to bones, while smooth muscles are involuntary and found in internal organs.

questions.question: Explain the features of cardiac muscles.
questions.answer: Cardiac muscles are branched, involuntary muscles found only in the heart and work continuously.

questions.question: Describe nervous tissue.
questions.answer: Nervous tissue receives stimuli and transmits nerve impulses to coordinate body activities.

questions.question: Explain the structure of a neuron.
questions.answer: A neuron consists of a cell body, dendrites and an axon which help transmit nerve impulses.

questions.question: What is the function of dendrites?
questions.answer: Dendrites receive nerve impulses from other neurons.

questions.question: What is the function of axon?
questions.answer: Axon carries nerve impulses away from the cell body.

questions.question: Explain the importance of nervous tissue in animals.
questions.answer: Nervous tissue helps in rapid communication and coordination of body functions.

questions.question: Why is blood considered a connective tissue?
questions.answer: Blood transports oxygen, nutrients and wastes and connects different parts of the body.

questions.question: Explain how muscle and nervous tissues work together.
questions.answer: Nervous tissue sends signals to muscles, causing them to contract and produce movement.
